item.title: 7 persistent claims about abortion, fact-checked
item.description:
According to the Pew Research Center's polls, 37% of Americans want abortion illegal in all or most cases. But an even bigger fraction — around 6 in 10 Americans — think abortion should be legal in all or most cases
Current abortion rates are lower than what they were in 1973 and are now less than half what they were at their peak in the early 1980s, according to the Guttmacher Institute, a reproductive health research organization that supports abortion rights.
The annual number of deaths related to legal induced abortion has fluctuated from year to year since 1973. Data from 2013 to 2018 showed the national case-fatality rate for legal induced abortion was 0.41 deaths per 100,000 legal induced abortions.
Trans and nonbinary people have undergone abortions as well. The Guttmacher Institute estimates in 2017, an estimated 462 to 530 transgender or nonbinary individuals in the U.S. had abortions.
"Most abortions in 2019 took place early in gestation," according to the CDC. Nearly 93% of abortions were performed at less than 13 weeks' gestation. Abortion pills, which can typically be used up to 10 weeks into a pregnancy, made up 54% of abortions in 2020.
Researchers wrote that fetal awareness of pain requires "functional thalamocortical connections." Those thalamocortical fibers begin appearing between 23 and 30 weeks' gestational age, but the capacity for pain perception comes later.
The argument against abortion has frequently been based on religion. Data shows that the majority of people who get an abortion have some sort of religious affiliation, according to the most recent Guttmacher Institute data, from 2014. Sauce: https://www.npr.org/2022/05/06/1096676197/7-persistent-claims-about-abortion-fact-checked?fbclid=IwAR2rF7SDeIzimDMHLnRctMnLHzly8Dx28dyUwgJ_nKNhZ-2d1INsjhHsOfo Front Page Edit: Send this to your favorite Pro-Lifer!! JK...kinda ;) *insert troll laughter here*

item.title: How to tell me you're a jackass without telling me you're a jackass
item.description: What a dipshit Graham is. Yeah, Lindsey, better pay, better working conditions, management responding to workers instead of dismissing them, not wanting to pee in a bottle because you don't have time while driving the Amazon delivery truck to take break - that's all about politics. Looks like Republican Union Hating will continue unabashed. Thanks for the poll boost, Lindsey, there are elections coming up. https://gizmodo.com/amazon-labor-union-leader-puts-lindsey-graham-in-his-pl-1848887996
item.dc:creator: RetrogradeLlama

item.title: How I met your mother.
item.dc:creator: WeirdAlisMySpiritUserName

item.title: In the light of all the madness I thought I'd share my story
item.description: In 2017 my wife and I were blessed with a child after about 6 months of trying. He was a very challenging pregnancy and my wife spent most of the 9 months being sick and fainting but he was worth it, especially for me as I had the easy part. Fast forward a year and we decided we wanted to try and have another one as I've always wanted 2 or 3 fairly close in age. We get pregnant fairly easily this time and all is going as expected until the 12 week scan when the ultrasound person goes really quiet and leaves the room to fetch senior midwives. After a barrage of tests, spread out over 10 weeks with just enough time for hope to build before it gets crushed again they are unable to tell us what condition our baby has but it is clearly incompatible with life as the baby is unable to move and not developing certain organs, primarily lungs, properly. We had to take a pill to stop our beautiful Aurora's heart and go in for her birth at the hospital. Leaving her behind when we left and went home was the worst thing I've ever had to do. They thought it was random gene anomaly and gave us a 5% chance of it happening again so a year later we decided to try. The same thing happened again with zephyr. During the 18 months the improvement in genome technology they were able to tell us what the issue was. By random chance the wife and I share a wonky gene, everyone has some and we just happened to have the same one. This condition now has a name and is always fatal as far as I'm aware. Now fortunately we're not going through this in the current climate of the USA (we're British so not impacted in the same way but this whole thing is horrific and scary). In the US as of now my wife would have had to carry our children to full term knowing they would never survive and the grief that would generate. I love all 3 of my children and would have done anything to have brought all of them home with me. I'm sorry for what you all are going through over there. Sorry its so long. Love you all ❤️

item.title: Post from 2015 still as relevant today. Issues never change.
item.dc:creator: pyroshen

item.title: I’m Hopeful but Terrified
item.description: I’ve been a quiet fence-sitter all my life, taking time to formulate my own opinions. However, putting my views out on a pedestal in public terrifies me. What if I get sent to jail or ostracized? I’m about to look for a job. What if my appearance there negatively affects my ability to find work? I live in a red state in the Bible Belt. That said, I think this cause may be worth it.
item.dc:creator: thethingthatwentsplat
